Minimum Experience and/or Education Requirements:

  

High school diploma or G.E.D. plus two or more years related experience (experience with federal employee benefits and compensation programs - particularly Legislative Branch programs, desirable) or equivalent combination of education and/or experience required.   

 

Preferred:

  

Completion of specialized training in relevant human resources subject areas, (e.g. federal benefits) which may be completed as part of college course work, independent courses or comparable on-the-job-training. 
Bachelor’s degree. 
SHRM, APA or OPM certification/accreditations.

 

Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check (or other applicable security clearance) and a pre-employment drug-test (pre-identified position only).

title: Communications Director
description_html:
Communications Director

Salary Range:  122,345.00 - 141,786.00

Closing Date:  3/20/2026

Job Summary:

The Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) provides operations support services and business solutions to the community of 10,000 House Members, Officers, and staff. The CAO organization comprises more than 800 technical and administrative staff working in a variety of areas, including information technology, finance, budget management, human resources, payroll, childcare, food and vending, procurement, logistics and administrative counsel.

This position is located in the Communications and Marketing Department, Immediate Office, Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO), U.S. House of Representatives (House). Whether it's creating a campaign to advertise the popular CAO House Staff Conferences, assisting a CAO department on messaging around their newest service for the House community, or guiding the editorial team through the Congressional Transition, the Communications Director will play a key role on the Communications and Marketing team and CAO at-large.

Working alongside the Creative Director, the Communications Director will keep a pulse on what the House campus is buzzing about, the latest trends, and creative new opportunities to reach House staff. The best fit for this role is someone who can work under tight deadlines while handling multiple projects, knows the power of data-centered campaigns, and wants to lead a highly creative editorial team.

Key responsibilities include supervising the editorial team; working closely with the Chief of Communications to create and execute high-priority communication plans for specific projects and customers; collaborating with the Creative Director to ensure alignment between the communications team and the design team.

The position has day-to-day supervisory/managerial responsibilities.

Primary Duties/Responsibilities:

·       Manages the communications team and monitor deadlines for all communications projects. This includes but is not limited to copy editing and strategic planning.

·       Coordinates with the Creative Director to ensure the communications and design team members are aligned on projects, deadlines, and other important information that could impact the deliverable(s).

·       Collaborates with leaders across the CAO to brand and market products and services; serves as a partner with CAO business units as they plan, develop, and implement new products and services for the House community.

·       Collaborates with the Communications Chief and Creative Director to identify opportunities for new communications initiatives. 

·       Collaborates with the editorial team to provide consultative services to CAO departments about their communications projects.

·       Develops, composes, and executes comprehensive communications plans for CAO services and initiatives targeted at increasing awareness and usage by Members, Committees, and Leadership.

·       Assists in the writing and copy editing of the CAO’s Semiannual Report.

·       Tracks and utilizes analytics for CAO communications.

·       Develops performance plans and evaluates employee performance.

·       Performs other official duties and special projects as assigned.


Qualifications:

Applicants must submit a resume and writing sample (e.g., a press release or blog post), and meet the following requirements:

Minimum:

·       Bachelor’s degree in Business, Communications, Advertising, Marketing; OR an equivalent combination of education and experience.

·       8-10 years of experience in marketing/communications with an advertising agency, on Capitol Hill, or within a marketing and communications department.

·       3-5 years of progressive management or leadership experience.

·       Experience with marketing campaign management.

·       Strong understanding of branding strategies.

·       Ability to understand analytics and collect data to drive communications decisions.

·       Knowledge of project management tools (e.g.: ClickUp, Monday, Trello, etc.)

·       All employees within CAO are expected to demonstrate the following general competencies: Accountability, Adaptability, Collaboration, Communication, Customer Orientation, Knowledge of Relevant Policies, Procedures, and Processes, Priority Management, and Problem Resolution.

*Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check (or other applicable security clearance) and a pre-employment drug-test (pre-identified position only).

title: Cloud Automation Engineer
description_html:
Cloud Automation Engineer

Salary Range:  155,689.00 - 163,349.00

Closing Date:  3/15/2026

Job Summary: 

 

The Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) provides operations support services and business solutions to the community of 10,000 House Members, Officers and staff. The CAO organization comprises more than 650 technical and administrative staff working in a variety of areas, including information technology, finance, budget management, human resources, payroll, childcare, food and vending, procurement, logistics and administrative counsel. 

  

The candidate must have a work experience in Data Storage Systems Administration, Enterprise Server operations, Data Center Operations, BC/DR, and Enterprise Data Protection and recovery s including Backups, Snapshots and Data Replication.  Duties include, but are not limited to, managing day-to-day Operations of the SAN/NAS/Object storage environments, new technology research/integration and heavy technical support. This position requires the candidate to be proficient in Storage provisioning and connectivity to a variety of systems (Physical and Virtual Server and OS platforms VMware, Unix, Windows & Kubernetes container environments). 

  

Candidates are required to work a diversified schedule and participate in 24x7 on-call support.  

  

This position has access to Personally Identifiable Information (PII). 

 

The position does not have day-to-day supervisory/managerial responsibilities. 

 

Primary Duties/Responsibilities: 


  • Deliver exceptional services to House via a stable, reliable and secure environment. Respond, diagnose and remediate issues affecting production availability in a dynamic environment  
  • Support day-to-day operations.  
  • Implement, configure and maintain SAN, NAS Storage and Cloud solutions in a Virtual and Physical servers’ environment; Conduct proof of concepts, Maintain environment with current releases of firmware and operating systems up to date 
  • Installs, maintains, configures, monitors, and supports all hardware systems and software components on the applicable platforms.  
  • Generate and compile Performance and Capacity reports and metrics.  
  • Must demonstrate willingness and aptitude to continually learn and adapt to new technologies. 
  • Position requires the ability to work shifts to provide technical support 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, participate in an On-Call rotation and be available OnDemand to address technical problems and support after-hours and weekend maintenance activities on a needed basis. This position requires occasional travel to multiple locations, as assigned. 
  • Demonstrate effective ticket queue etiquette by consistently resolving tickets in a timely manner and communicate promptly using email. Maintain accurate and precise ticket notes within each assigned service ticket that effectively relay what occurred while supporting the ticket and the outcome and/or path forward. 
  • Troubleshooting and analytical skills; ability to anticipate and resolve technical problems in a Storage and Server environment. 
  • Collaborate and Interact with other internal HIR Teams and external vendors as assigned. Act as a facilitator for team meetings on a rotating basis, as assigned. 
  • Perform training sessions that will be resourceful and provide additional knowledge on a variety of technical topics for peers. 
  • Develop, maintain and assist in writing, structuring, and organizing technical documentation and standard operating procedures 
  • Provides technical advice to management regarding future and directions as server and network technologies continue to evolve 
  • Acts as an internal consultant providing technical guidance on the most complex projects; regularly provides technical leadership to colleagues, assisting them with successfully completing assigned projects. 
  • Plans and recommendations for changes to the configuration and capacity/performance of the operating system. 
  • Prepares long and short-range plans for systems maintenance, production activities, and for necessary support resources. 
  • Ensures computer systems security issues are addressed effectively in the House environment for all components of the platform operating system environment (e.g., network, desktop, Internet).  
  • Demonstrates willingness and cooperation in participating in matrix managed projects. 
  • Maintains professional relationships with customers, management, colleagues, and vendors while delivers high-quality customer service. 
  • Provides input to management, as requested, on project team performance for Individual Performance Plans and employee performance evaluations.  
  • Performs other official duties and projects assigned. 


Minimum Requirements: 


  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science/engineering, Information Systems, Information Technology, degree with 5+ years of relevant storage experience as an Information Technology professional in an organization. 

   

Knowledge, Skill, and Ability (KSA) and Competency Requirements:  

  

  • Possess 5+ years of experience in Storage administration of SAN, NAS, Storage and Data Protection technologies, Architectures, Design, Administration and Support of Storage systems in a data center environment. 
  • Knowledge of Data and Storage system Security, Web Applications, Databases, VMware, Windows, UNIX servers and Kubernetes Containers.  
  • Knowledge of Local Area Networks, Wide Area networks, Operating systems, Storage Area Network (SAN), Network Attached Storage (NAS) and backup solutions. 
  • Knowledge of Network File share Administration CIFS and NFS  
  • Knowledge in Cloud Storage connectivity, Tiering to Cloud  
  • Expertise in Enterprise level NetApp, Pure Storage systems/arrays and Cisco MDS SAN switches is preferred. 
  • Knowledge of Microsoft File NAS. Administration of File share and Windows NTFS Folder and File permissions, access, ACLs in an Active Directory environment 
  • Ability to develop and deploy customized solutions 
  • Certification in Storage Systems is preferred. Possessing certification in other storage and cloud technologies is a plus 
  • All employees within CAO are expected to demonstrate the following general competencies: Accountability, Adaptability, Collaboration, Communication, Customer Orientation, Knowledge of Relevant Policies, Procedures, and Processes, Priority Management, and Problem Resolution. 
  • Ability to communicate effectively, both orally and in writing, with peers, senior staff, information systems professionals, and the user community 
  •  A strong desire to learn and expand on existing knowledge is a requirement. Ability to grasp fundamentals of new technologies quickly and employ in organization.  
  • Knowledge on Automation of Storage administration tasks and jobs using tools 
  • Troubleshooting and analytical skills; ability to anticipate and resolve technical problems 
  • Ability to develop solutions to complex technical problems.  
  • Knowledge of platform specific operating systems and associated products.  
  • Knowledge of all phases of projects, systems analysis and administration. 

 

Security Requirements: 

 

  • This position requires unescorted access to secure offsite locations which require an identification badge. In order to be approved for the applicable badge, you must be a U.S. citizen. 

 

  • This position requires that the applicant obtain and maintain an applicable U.S. Government security clearance, which requires U.S. citizenship. We will not submit your application for a clearance unless you are a U.S. citizen. 

  

**Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check (or other applicable security clearance, a pre-employment drug-test) and a probationary period of 2 years.

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science/engineering, Information Systems, Information Technology, degree with 5+ years of relevant storage experience as an Information Technology professional in an organization. 

   

Knowledge, Skill, and Ability (KSA) and Competency Requirements:  

  

  • Possess 5+ years of experience in Storage administration of SAN, NAS, Storage and Data Protection technologies, Architectures, Design, Administration and Support of Storage systems in a data center environment. 
  • Knowledge of Data and Storage system Security, Web Applications, Databases, VMware, Windows, UNIX servers and Kubernetes Containers.  
  • Knowledge of Local Area Networks, Wide Area networks, Operating systems, Storage Area Network (SAN), Network Attached Storage (NAS) and backup solutions. 
  • Knowledge of Network File share Administration CIFS and NFS  
  • Knowledge in Cloud Storage connectivity, Tiering to Cloud  
  • Expertise in Enterprise level NetApp, Pure Storage systems/arrays and Cisco MDS SAN switches is preferred. 
  • Knowledge of Microsoft File NAS. Administration of File share and Windows NTFS Folder and File permissions, access, ACLs in an Active Directory environment 
  • Ability to develop and deploy customized solutions 
  • Certification in Storage Systems is preferred. Possessing certification in other storage and cloud technologies is a plus 
  • All employees within CAO are expected to demonstrate the following general competencies: Accountability, Adaptability, Collaboration, Communication, Customer Orientation, Knowledge of Relevant Policies, Procedures, and Processes, Priority Management, and Problem Resolution. 
  • Ability to communicate effectively, both orally and in writing, with peers, senior staff, information systems professionals, and the user community 
  •  A strong desire to learn and expand on existing knowledge is a requirement. Ability to grasp fundamentals of new technologies quickly and employ in organization.  
  • Knowledge on Automation of Storage administration tasks and jobs using tools 
  • Troubleshooting and analytical skills; ability to anticipate and resolve technical problems 
  • Ability to develop solutions to complex technical problems.  
  • Knowledge of platform specific operating systems and associated products.  
  • Knowledge of all phases of projects, systems analysis and administration. 

 

Security Requirements: 

 

  • This position requires unescorted access to secure offsite locations which require an identification badge. In order to be approved for the applicable badge, you must be a U.S. citizen. 

 

  • This position requires that the applicant obtain and maintain an applicable U.S. Government security clearance, which requires U.S. citizenship. We will not submit your application for a clearance unless you are a U.S. citizen. 

  

**Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check (or other applicable security clearance, a pre-employment drug-test) and a probationary period of 2 years.

This position requires the candidate to be proficient in Storage provisioning and connectivity to a variety of systems (Physical and Virtual Server and OS platforms VMware, Unix, Windows & Kubernetes container environments). 

  

Candidates are required to work a diversified schedule and participate in 24x7 on-call support.

Generate and compile Performance and Capacity reports and metrics.

title: Senior Project Designer
description_html:
Senior Project Designer

Salary Range:  84,271.00 - 92,783.00

Closing Date:  3/12/2026

Job Summary:

The Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) provides operations support services and business solutions to the community of 10,000 House Members, Officers and staff. The CAO organization comprises more than 800 technical and administrative staff working in a variety of areas, including information technology, finance, budget management, human resources, payroll, childcare, food and vending, procurement, logistics and administrative counsel.

The position is with House Interior Design, Office of Logistics & Support (L&S), Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO), U.S. House of Representatives (House). The purpose of the Office of L&S is to provide important logistical services to the entire House community, including building, repairing, moving, setting up, and breaking down furniture; facilitating office moves; and managing all accountable equipment assets within the House. The purpose of House Interior Design is to serve as the main point of contact for Leadership, Committee and Member office design, specializing in design consultation services using Antique and Unique, Core and Modular furniture from the House inventory as well as special order furnishings to support Finish Schedule projects. This position serves as a Senior Project Designer.

Key responsibilities include planning, managing, and monitoring House Interior Design projects; tracking project progress; coordinating with other personnel both internal and external in support of project progress; creating accurate furniture plans, 3D renderings and visually effective presentations; providing excellent and consistent customer service; serve as an expert in furniture provided by the House, and as a mentor and trainer to colleagues. 

The position does not have day-to-day supervisory/managerial responsibilities.

Primary Duties/Responsibilities:

  • Provides interior design services to Members, Committees, and House support offices; applies skills and experience to plan, manage, and monitor House related furniture projects, office consultations, and renovations.
  • Assists with multiple projects across varying types of design (A&U, Finish Schedule and/or Modular) and can shift priorities quickly as needed to support MCL requests.
  • Helps manage incoming requests. Answers routine inquiries and ensures that adequate office coverage is maintained during official business hours. Delivers excellent customer service; ensures the availability of support services and provides accurate and timely information.
  • Establishes and maintains good working relationships with key stakeholders and customers; establishes credibility in all interactions. Coordinates with other individuals/teams within the Office of Logistics and Support, CAO and external agencies (AOC, USCP, Clerk, and HSAA) in support of project completion.
  • Leads by overseeing and monitoring projects that include floor plans, carpet and drape selections; provides expert assistance and adept guidance in administering, managing, and evaluating these projects. Demonstrates excellent project management skills to include proactive customer engagement and professional written and verbal communication to facilitate project coordination, customer satisfaction, problem resolution, and schedule tracking.
  • Uses architectural plans to complete office designs; provides guidance and insight on floor plans, item placement, and the usage of various materials; applies industry standards for product specification appropriate for contract/government interiors; applies design recommendation in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA).
  • Creates realistic 3D renderings incorporating accurate room details using proposed patterns, colors, and finishes.
  • Evaluates and assesses House furniture assets for condition and required shop-work; collaborates with shops to write corresponding tickets to perform refurbishment and delivery.
  • Applies knowledge of high quality, American-made commercial, residential and/or hospitality sources for furniture, carpet, and drapery applicable to finish schedule or other special projects.
  • Conducts vendor outreach and coordination for price quotes, material reserves, and site visits as required to inspect furniture for quality and order accuracy.
  • Reviews vendor quotes/purchase orders to ensure accuracy and compliance; takes corrective action to rectify problems or prepares recommendations, as needed.
  • Assist with the curation and evaluation of product lines based on quality, durability, popularity and ease of in-house maintenance and repair.
  • Distributes various communications to customers, stakeholders, and staff regarding deadlines, policies, business process changes, and other department specific guidelines related to design services.
  • Presents results, status reports, and analyses to management, including metric reports based on project schedule.
  • Understands and adheres to policies, procedures, and processes; aids others as needed.
  • Demonstrates knowledge of the formal and informal structures and workflow within the organization.
  • Demonstrates an understanding of the legislative environment and the operational practices of the House.
  • Serves as the Contracting Officer Representative (COR) for design resource contracts.
  • Assists in planning and executing activities related to the bi-annual Congressional transition and determining resource needs.
  • Maintains and manages records as appropriate and in accordance with relevant policies and procedures.
  • Recommends improvements to policies, procedures, and processes; assists in implementing improvements to enhance efficiency and effectiveness.
  • Promotes an environment of coaching, teamwork, creative thinking, and support.
  • Performs other official duties and special projects as assigned.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Interior Design, Interior Architecture or related field. 
  • Minimum of three (3) years of relevant experience.
  • Software competency in AutoCAD (or similar CAD software for space planning) and SketchUp, Lumion, CET designer (or similar 3D rendering software).
  • Demonstrated competency in written and oral presentation skills and supporting software such as Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative suite, etc.
  • Proficient in project management skills, especially workload prioritization and customer service.
  • Knowledge of SharePoint, ClickUp, and Microsoft Project is preferred.
  • Portfolio demonstrating interior design experience including furnishing sourcing and 3D rendering skills. 
  • Work experience within the legislative branch and the House environment (e.g., Capitol Hill, House customers).
  • Knowledge of the design operations and services offered by the Office of Logistics and Support. 
  • Adept in communicating with high-level stakeholders, customers, and leadership to discuss office designs and relocation efforts. 
  • Ability to manage highly confidential and sensitive information appropriately.

Preferred Qualifications 

  • Certifications in NCIDQ or Interior Design
  • ASID or IIDA membership
  • Knowledge of project management software, (e.g. sharepoint, click up)

Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check (or other applicable security clearance) and a pre-employment drug-test (pre-identified position only).

title: Senior Systems Engineer (Linux Systems Admin)
description_html:
Senior Systems Engineer (Linux Systems Admin)

Salary Range:  122,345.00 - 154,171.00

Closing Date:  3/17/2026

Job Summary:

The Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) provides operations support services and business solutions to the community of 10,000 House Members, Officers and staff. The CAO organization comprises more than 800 technical and administrative staff working in a variety of areas, including information technology, finance, budget management, human resources, payroll, child care, food and vending, procurement, logistics and administrative counsel.

We are looking for a Linux System Administrator to join our dynamic team of system engineers who are responsible for designing, implementing, and monitoring our Linux infrastructure. This position is located in the Enterprise Infrastructure Directorate, House Information Resources (HIR), Office of the Chief Administrative Officer (CAO), U.S. House of Representatives (House). The Enterprise Infrastructure Directorate provides engineering and technical support in the following areas: Voice Communications, Data Center Operations, Server and Storage Management, and Administrative Support. The teams within this Directorate support the infrastructure (circuits, cabling, servers, data storage, voice systems, and other physical infrastructure) that provides computing and telecommunications services to the House. This position serves as the Senior Systems Engineer.

Key responsibilities include serving as a key technical resource and senior advisor to management on all technical issues related to the sustainment of various server platforms supporting applications of the House; planning, organizing, installing, and monitoring large and complex technical projects; producing technical solutions to complex problems; and providing leadership and technical guidance to colleagues, assisting them with successfully completing assigned projects.

 The position does not have day-to-day supervisory/managerial responsibilities.

Primary Duties/Responsibilities:

  • Proactively maintain and develop Linux infrastructure technology to maintain a 24x7x365 uptime service
  • Engineer systems administration related solutions for various project and operational needs
  • Maintain best practices on managing systems and services across all environments
  • Ensures systems follow security guidelines and standards as well as perform and analyze on going security audits
  • Research, diagnose and resolve complex computer system software and hardware issues as they arise
  • Proactively monitoring system performance and perform capacity planning
  • Manage, coordinate, and implement software upgrades, patches, errata and hot fixes as needed
  • Create and modify scripts or applications to perform tasks
  • Provide input on ways to improve the stability, security, efficiency, and scalability of the server environment
  • Collaborate with other teams and team members to develop automation strategies and deployment processes
  • Participate in an On-Call rotation
  • Performs other official duties as assigned.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum 5 years of relevant experience with Previous working experience as a Linux Administrator
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or similar relevant degree
  • In depth command line knowledge and troubleshooting of Red Hat OS version 8+ operating systems
  • Experience with Red Hat Satellite, Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform and Red Hat Open Shift
  • Familiarity with monitoring tools such as Dynatrace, Nagios Core and Nagios Log Monitoring
  • Ability to multi-task
  • Experienced in writing shell scripts
  • Strong problem solving and communication skills
  • Familiarity with IT management concepts of Virtualization, Containerization, Data Storage, SAN & Data Protection within an enterprise operations environment
  • Solid knowledge of protocols such as DNS, HTTP, LDAP, SMTP and SNMP
  • Familiarity with container management tools such as Podman

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Certifications in - Linux, RHCSA, RHCE, Red hat certified Specialist in Containers and Kubernetes or Red Hat Certified Specialist in OpenShift Administration and AWS

Security Requirements:
This position requires unescorted access to secure offsite locations which require an identification badge. In order to be approved for the applicable badge, you must be a U.S. citizen.

Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check (or other applicable security clearance) and a pre-employment drug-test (pre-identified position only).

title: Editor (Scopist)
description_html:
Editor (Scopist)

Salary:  Up to $95,995.00

Closing Date:  Open Until Filled


Job Summary
Responsible for editing substantially verbatim transcripts prepared by Official Reporters to produce a precise and properly formatted record of House of Representatives proceedings. Requires an understanding of legislative and parliamentary terminology. Uses computer-aided transcription (CAT) software to meet strict deadlines while adhering to House policies and procedures. Work is performed at a compact workstation in a shared office setting with moderate ambient noise and limited privacy.

Duties
• Works cooperatively and courteously with colleagues and handles stress appropriately.

• Edits files prepared by Official Reporters, ensuring accuracy, required formatting, and use of proper grammar, punctuation, and style consistent with House standards, Office of Official Reporters practices, and the Government Publishing Office (GPO) Style Manual.

• Ensures the use of appropriate parliamentary language consistent with House rules and procedures to accurately reflect proceedings.

• Confirms names, quoted material, and terminology to ensure an accurate record.

• Coordinates with Official Reporters and fellow editors to resolve inconsistencies, incorporate revisions, and maintain consistency.

• Maintains working knowledge of House Floor and committee operations, legislative and parliamentary terminology, and House procedures.

• Meets daily production requirements, including editing a minimum of 125 pages per day.

• Incorporates supervisory feedback and meets attendance expectations.

• Ability to work a condensed work week with several late-night hours when the House is in session.

• Provides professional, tactful service and supports additional Office of Official Reporters functions as needed.

Qualifications
• Ability to perform the duties described above and to exercise discretion and independent judgment.

• At least 5 years of experience editing verbatim transcripts is strongly preferred; equivalent experience demonstrating the ability to perform the above duties may be accepted in lieu of this requirement.

• Expert knowledge of grammar, punctuation, spelling, and vocabulary.

• Familiarity with legislative procedures, parliamentary terminology, and congressional operations (preferred; willingness to learn required).

• Knowledge of the Government Publishing Office (GPO) and institutional formatting standards (preferred; willingness to learn required).

• Skill in using Windows-based systems and CAT software (preferred; willingness to learn required).

• Ability to work extended, irregular, and extended hours as required by House activity.

• Demonstrated reliability, dependability, and consistent attendance.

• Ability to obtain and maintain a national security clearance.

Instructions
No phone calls please. Interested candidates should send a cover letter and resume to: Damien Jackson, Office of Official Reporters (damien.jackson@mail.house.gov), Subject Line: House of Representatives Editor

title: Official Reporter (Stenographer)
description_html:
Official Reporter (Stenographer)

Salary:  Up to $157,291.00

Closing Date:  Open Until Filled


Job Summary
Responsible for verbatim reporting of congressional proceedings, floor activities, meetings, hearings, markups, and caucus meetings; responsible for the general understanding of legislative and parliamentary nuances related to the Congressional Record; produce transcripts in a timely fashion while maintaining the highest degree of confidentiality of secret and top-secret documents. Works in a small workstation in an office environment without expectation of privacy where noise levels are usually moderate.

1)  Accurate reporting of congressional proceedings, floor activities, hearings, and caucus meetings.

2)  Utilization of proper English grammar and punctuation in accordance with the Government Publishing Office Style Manual.

3)  Use of parliamentary language consistent with committee rules procedures to reflect actions taken by Congress.

4)  Edit and proofread transcripts.

5)  Collect and insert all appropriate documents from proceedings to reflect actions taken.
 
6)  Understands and supports other services and areas of the Office of Official Reporters.

7)  Accepts performance-based criticism and direction, meets attendance requirements as established by the office, and works a flexible schedule including long hours, nights, and weekends when necessary.

8)  Maintains a good working relationship with other Office of Official Reporters staff, works cooperatively and courteously with others, works well under pressure, and handles stress appropriately.

9)  Performs other official duties as assigned.

Qualifications
Temperament to communicate with a variety of personalities in a tactful, pleasant, and professional manner. Ability to perform the major duties described above. Ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in fulfillment of responsibilities. Knowledge of office policies, practices, and procedures. Strong organizational skills required. Extensive knowledge and experience in reporting and ability to report multi-voice hearings on various subject matters at very high speeds.  Expert knowledge of English spelling and vocabulary and the Rules and procedures of the House. A National Court Reporters Association (NCRA) proficiency certification or similar certification from any State or jurisdiction is strongly preferred. National Security Clearance is required. This position is identified as a Test Designated Position and is subject to random drug testing. Must have familiarity with the congressional legislative process, have an analytical mind, and be detail oriented.

Instructions
No phone calls please. Interested candidates should send a cover letter and resume to: Damien Jackson, Office of Official Reporters (damien.jackson@mail.house.gov), Subject Line: Official Reporter.

1)  Accurate reporting of congressional proceedings, floor activities, hearings, and caucus meetings.

2)  Utilization of proper English grammar and punctuation in accordance with the Government Publishing Office Style Manual.

3)  Use of parliamentary language consistent with committee rules procedures to reflect actions taken by Congress.

4)  Edit and proofread transcripts.

5)  Collect and insert all appropriate documents from proceedings to reflect actions taken.
 
6)  Understands and supports other services and areas of the Office of Official Reporters.

7)  Accepts performance-based criticism and direction, meets attendance requirements as established by the office, and works a flexible schedule including long hours, nights, and weekends when necessary.

8)  Maintains a good working relationship with other Office of Official Reporters staff, works cooperatively and courteously with others, works well under pressure, and handles stress appropriately.

9)  Performs other official duties as assigned.

Qualifications
Temperament to communicate with a variety of personalities in a tactful, pleasant, and professional manner. Ability to perform the major duties described above. Ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in fulfillment of responsibilities. Knowledge of office policies, practices, and procedures. Strong organizational skills required. Extensive knowledge and experience in reporting and ability to report multi-voice hearings on various subject matters at very high speeds.  Expert knowledge of English spelling and vocabulary and the Rules and procedures of the House. A National Court Reporters Association (NCRA) proficiency certification or similar certification from any State or jurisdiction is strongly preferred. National Security Clearance is required. This position is identified as a Test Designated Position and is subject to random drug testing. Must have familiarity with the congressional legislative process, have an analytical mind, and be detail oriented.

Instructions
No phone calls please. Interested candidates should send a cover letter and resume to: Damien Jackson, Office of Official Reporters (damien.jackson@mail.house.gov), Subject Line: Official Reporter.

title: Senior IT Business and Systems Analyst
description_html:
Senior IT Business and Systems Analyst

Closing Date: Position is Open until filled
Salary Range: 120,000.00 - 144,000.00

The Office of the Clerk serves as the official record keeper of the US House of Representatives, and supports the nonpartisan legislative functions of the institution, including disseminating legislative information and supporting technological advancement. With an abiding dedication to service, transparency, and accessibility Legislative Computer Systems is building the next generation of applications to support this mission and provide information to our government partners and to the public.

The Legislative Computer Systems (LCS) division within the Office of the Clerk, U.S. House of Representatives is seeking a Senior IT Systems and Business Analyst to join our Quality Assurance team. This role operates within an enterprise IT environment that includes software engineers, DevOps specialists, testers, contractors and other business analysts. LCS follows Agile delivery practices with frequent releases, continuous integration and deployment, and a strong emphasis on incorporating customer feedback into ongoing development.

The selected candidate will be responsible for developing, analyzing, and maintaining business and system requirements, user stories, and related artifacts across multiple systems. They will work within an Agile product team alongside testers, other business analysts, developers, and engineers to ensure user needs are clearly understood and translated into clear, complete, and testable system specifications. The position supports the full lifecycle of software delivery, including requirements refinement, development, testing, deployment, and user adoption, and provides support for product ownership and project management–related activities as needed. The role plays a critical part in ensuring requirements readiness, stakeholder alignment, and delivery clarity for mission-critical systems operating under legislative timelines.

This is a hybrid position with required on-site work on Capitol Hill, Washington, DC. Telework is available. Occasional work outside normal business hours may be required due to the operational needs of the House.

PRIMARY DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES

·         Develop, analyze, and maintain business and system requirements, user stories, and related artifacts across multiple systems.

·         Translate user and stakeholder needs into clear, complete, and testable functional and technical specifications, including acceptance criteria.

·         Work alongside testers, developers, engineers, and other analysts to support effective planning, refinement, and product delivery.

·         Support the full lifecycle of software delivery, including requirements refinement, development, testing, deployment, and user adoption.

·         Coordinate and support user acceptance testing and communicate requirement-related risks or impacts as needed.

·         Provide training and support for new and existing systems and applications

·             ·        Work in a collaborative environment across multiple teams and multiple projects.


QUALIFICATIONS

·         Seven (7) or more years of experience in an IT-related field, including significant experience in business or systems analysis.

·         Demonstrated experience eliciting, analyzing, and documenting business and technical requirements for IT development projects.

·         Demonstrated experience translating stakeholder needs into functional and technical specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria.

·         Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, including the ability to evaluate business needs, system impacts, and tradeoffs.

·         Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to communicate requirements, impacts, and priorities to technical and non-technical audiences.

·         Ability to manage competing priorities and work effectively under pressure in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

It would be ideal if your experience also includes:

·         Experience supporting backlog refinement, user acceptance testing, and release planning.

·         Familiarity with data-driven or integrated enterprise systems.

·         Experience working in Agile delivery environments.

·         Knowledge of the federal legislative and law-making process.

·         Knowledge of project management and product ownership practices, including experience aligned with PMP and/or Agile Product Owner or ScrumMaster frameworks

Hiring for this position is governed by the Veterans Employment Opportunities Act. Applicants seeking veterans’ preference consideration should email veoa-iohr@mail.house.gov for more information.

Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check. Employment with the U.S. House of Representatives is at-will. EOE.

title: Senior Test Analyst
description_html:
Senior Test Analyst

Closing Date: Position is Open until filled
Salary Range: 120,000.00 - 144,000.00

The Office of the Clerk serves as the official record keeper of the US House of Representatives, and supports the nonpartisan legislative functions of the institution, including disseminating legislative information and supporting technological advancement. With an abiding dedication to service, transparency, and accessibility Legislative Computer Systems is building the next generation of applications to support this mission and provide information to our government partners and to the public.

The Legislative Computer Systems (LCS) division within the Office of the Clerk, U.S. House of Representatives is seeking a Senior Test Analyst to join our Quality Assurance team. This role operates within an enterprise IT environment that includes software engineers, DevOps specialists, testers, contractors, business analysts, and other manual and automated testers. LCS follows Agile delivery practices with frequent releases, continuous integration and deployment, and a strong emphasis on incorporating customer feedback into ongoing development.

The selected candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and executing automated and manual tests to ensure software applications and data-driven systems meet functional, technical, performance, and security requirements. They will build and maintain automated test suites within Azure DevOps and CI/CD pipelines to support continuous integration, regression testing, and high-quality software releases. They will work within an Agile product team alongside business analysts, other testers, developers, and engineers to ensure quality standards, testability, and delivery timelines are met. The role plays a critical part in supporting release readiness for mission-critical systems operating under legislative timelines.

This is a hybrid position with required on-site work on Capitol Hill, Washington, DC. Telework is available. Occasional work outside normal business hours may be required due to the operational needs of the House.

PRIMARY DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES

·         Design, develop, and execute automated and manual tests to validate that system requirements and acceptance criteria are met.

·         Build and maintain automated test suites within Azure DevOps and CI/CD pipelines to support continuous integration, regression testing, release readiness, and product delivery.

·         Work alongside business analysts, developers, engineers, and other testers to ensure quality standards and testability are met.

·         Analyze and communicate test results, defects, trends, and quality risks to technical and non-technical audiences.

·         Support quality assurance and user acceptance testing across multiple projects.

·         Support the full lifecycle of software delivery, including requirements refinement, development, testing, deployment, and user adoption.

·        Work in a collaborative environment across multiple teams and multiple projects.


·         Seven (7) or more years of experience in an IT-related field, including significant hands-on experience in software testing or quality assurance.

·         Demonstrated experience designing, developing, and executing automated and manual tests for complex software applications and data-driven systems.

·         Hands-on experience with test automation tools and integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines.

·         Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, including the ability to assess system behavior, identify defects, and evaluate quality and release risks.

·         Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to clearly communicate testing results, risks, and impacts to technical and non-technical audiences.

·         Ability to manage competing priorities and work effectively under pressure in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

It would be ideal if your experience also includes:

·         Experience with Azure DevOps and modern test automation frameworks.

·         Familiarity with data-driven or integrated enterprise systems.

·         Experience supporting regression testing, release readiness, and quality reporting.

·         Experience working in Agile delivery environments.

·         Knowledge of the federal legislative and law-making process.


Hiring for this position is governed by the Veterans Employment Opportunities Act. Applicants seeking veterans’ preference consideration should email veoa-iohr@mail.house.gov for more information.

Continued employment is contingent upon satisfactorily completing a criminal history records check. Employment with the U.S. House of Representatives is at-will. EOE.

title: Green & Gold Congressional Aide
description_html:
Green & Gold Congressional Aide
SALARY: Salary $56,000.00 - 68,000.00
LOCATION: JUPITER FL

The Green & Gold Congressional Aide Program (G&G CAP) was established to create employment opportunities for Veterans, Gold Star families, and Active-Duty spouses within the U.S. House of Representatives. These positions provide Aides with the experience and exposure necessary to broaden their career opportunities. G&G CAP Aides may be assigned to Members, Committees, or House Officers and will serve in Washington, DC or Member district offices across the country. These are two-year, paid, full-time employment opportunities.

Duties will vary depending upon the specific requirements for each office. Said duties may include but are not limited to:

  • Working as a constituent services representative;
  • Helping local constituents resolve issues with federal agencies;
  • Serving as liaison to local Veterans Service Organizations;
  • Attending local events and meetings on behalf of the Member of Congress; and
  • Performing legislative work.

SPECIFIC DUTIES FOR EACH OFFICE WILL BE DISCUSSED DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS. 

IN ORDER TO PARTICIPATE YOU MUST BE A VETERAN, GOLD STAR FAMILY MEMBER, OR ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSE AND MEET ALL THE FOLLOWING REQUIREMENTS. NO WAIVERS WILL BE GRANTED:

Veterans:

1.  Honorably discharged;

2.  Released from active duty within the last six years;

3.  Pay grades at or below E-5/O-3/W-2;

  • Veterans promoted to the pay grades of E-6/O-4/W-3 WITHIN 6-MONTHS OF SEPARATION from active duty may be eligible if they meet ALL other requirements.

4.  Veterans who are in receipt of a 20-year or Temporary Early Retirement Authority (TERA)     retirement are not eligible for the program.

Gold Star Families: 

  • A surviving spouse, child/step-child, parent/step-parent, or sibling/step-sibling of a veteran who died:
    • In the line of duty while serving in the Armed Forces; or
    • from a service-connected disability.

Active-Duty Spouses: 

  • Must be the spouse of an Active-Duty service member. Active-Duty service members work for the military full-time and are subject to permanent change of station or permanent change of assignment orders upon completion of each tour of duty.
    • Those subject to Title X mobilization are not eligible

Additional Requirements
Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. Ability to work well under pressure and maintain a professional and courteous work disposition; ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in handling sensitive information; ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. High School Diploma required.  Effective verbal and written communication skills are necessary.

Required Documents
VETERANS must submit a Resume and DD-214 (Member COPY 4)

GOLD STAR applicants require a Resume and one of the following:
**For members who died on active duty: DD-1300 or DD-2064, accompanied by a Line of Duty Determination; OR
**For members who died of service-connected disability: Last DD-214 issued to the veteran, and VA eligibility letter.
**Proof of dependency on the Veteran: DD-1172 (PAGE 2); OR
**Proof of relationship (obituary, birth certificate, etc…) to the Veteran.

ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSES must submit a Resume and a copy of the FRONT and BACK of Dependent ID card

Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.

The Green & Gold Congressional Aide Program (G&G CAP) was established to create employment opportunities for Veterans, Gold Star families, and Active-Duty spouses within the U.S. House of Representatives. These positions provide Aides with the experience and exposure necessary to broaden their career opportunities. G&G CAP Aides may be assigned to Members, Committees, or House Officers and will serve in Washington, DC or Member district offices across the country. **For members who died on active duty: DD-1300 or DD-2064, accompanied by a Line of Duty Determination; OR
**For members who died of service-connected disability: Last DD-214 issued to the veteran, and VA eligibility letter.
**Proof of dependency on the Veteran: DD-1172 (PAGE 2); OR
**Proof of relationship (obituary, birth certificate, etc…) to the Veteran.

ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSES must submit a Resume and a copy of the FRONT and BACK of Dependent ID card



Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.

title: Green & Gold Congressional Aide
description_html:
Green & Gold Congressional Aide

The Green & Gold Congressional Aide Program (G&G CAP) was established to create employment opportunities for Veterans, Gold Star families, and Active-Duty spouses within the U.S. House of Representatives. These positions provide Aides with the experience and exposure necessary to broaden their career opportunities. G&G CAP Aides may be assigned to Members, Committees, or House Officers and will serve in Washington, DC or Member district offices across the country. These are two-year, paid, full-time employment opportunities.

Duties will vary depending upon the specific requirements for each office. Said duties may include but are not limited to:

  • Working as a constituent services representative;
  • Helping local constituents resolve issues with federal agencies;
  • Serving as liaison to local Veterans Service Organizations;
  • Attending local events and meetings on behalf of the Member of Congress; and
  • Performing legislative work.

SPECIFIC DUTIES FOR EACH OFFICE WILL BE DISCUSSED DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS. 

IN ORDER TO PARTICIPATE YOU MUST BE A VETERAN, GOLD STAR FAMILY MEMBER, OR ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSE AND MEET ALL THE FOLLOWING REQUIREMENTS. NO WAIVERS WILL BE GRANTED:

Veterans:

1.  Honorably discharged;

2.  Released from active duty within the last six years;

3.  Pay grades at or below E-5/O-3/W-2;

  • Veterans promoted to the pay grades of E-6/O-4/W-3 WITHIN 6-MONTHS OF SEPARATION from active duty may be eligible if they meet ALL other requirements.

4.  Veterans who are in receipt of a 20-year or Temporary Early Retirement Authority (TERA)     retirement are not eligible for the program.

Gold Star Families: 

  • A surviving spouse, child/step-child, parent/step-parent, or sibling/step-sibling of a veteran who died:
    • In the line of duty while serving in the Armed Forces; or
    • from a service-connected disability.

Active-Duty Spouses: 

  • Must be the spouse of an Active-Duty service member. Active-Duty service members work for the military full-time and are subject to permanent change of station or permanent change of assignment orders upon completion of each tour of duty.
    • Those subject to Title X mobilization are not eligible

Additional Requirements
Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. Ability to work well under pressure and maintain a professional and courteous work disposition; ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in handling sensitive information; ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. High School Diploma required.  Effective verbal and written communication skills are necessary.

Required Documents
VETERANS must submit a Resume and DD-214 (Member COPY 4)

GOLD STAR applicants require a Resume and one of the following:
**For members who died on active duty: DD-1300 or DD-2064, accompanied by a Line of Duty Determination; OR
**For members who died of service-connected disability: Last DD-214 issued to the veteran, and VA eligibility letter.
**Proof of dependency on the Veteran: DD-1172 (PAGE 2); OR
**Proof of relationship (obituary, birth certificate, etc…) to the Veteran.

ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSES must submit a Resume and a copy of the FRONT and BACK of Dependent ID card

Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.

Green & Gold Congressional Aide
SALARY: Salary $56,000.00 - 68,000.00
LOCATION: NEWNAN GA

The Green & Gold Congressional Aide Program (G&G CAP) was established to create employment opportunities for Veterans, Gold Star families, and Active-Duty spouses within the U.S. House of Representatives. These positions provide Aides with the experience and exposure necessary to broaden their career opportunities. G&G CAP Aides may be assigned to Members, Committees, or House Officers and will serve in Washington, DC or Member district offices across the country. These are two-year, paid, full-time employment opportunities.

Duties will vary depending upon the specific requirements for each office. Said duties may include but are not limited to:

  • Working as a constituent services representative;
  • Helping local constituents resolve issues with federal agencies;
  • Serving as liaison to local Veterans Service Organizations;
  • Attending local events and meetings on behalf of the Member of Congress; and
  • Performing legislative work.

SPECIFIC DUTIES FOR EACH OFFICE WILL BE DISCUSSED DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS. 

IN ORDER TO PARTICIPATE YOU MUST BE A VETERAN, GOLD STAR FAMILY MEMBER, OR ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSE AND MEET ALL THE FOLLOWING REQUIREMENTS. NO WAIVERS WILL BE GRANTED:

Veterans:

1.  Honorably discharged;

2.  Released from active duty within the last six years;

3.  Pay grades at or below E-5/O-3/W-2;

  • Veterans promoted to the pay grades of E-6/O-4/W-3 WITHIN 6-MONTHS OF SEPARATION from active duty may be eligible if they meet ALL other requirements.

4.  Veterans who are in receipt of a 20-year or Temporary Early Retirement Authority (TERA)     retirement are not eligible for the program.

Gold Star Families: 

  • A surviving spouse, child/step-child, parent/step-parent, or sibling/step-sibling of a veteran who died:
    • In the line of duty while serving in the Armed Forces; or
    • from a service-connected disability.

  • Must be the spouse of an Active-Duty service member. Active-Duty service members work for the military full-time and are subject to permanent change of station or permanent change of assignment orders upon completion of each tour of duty.

Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. Ability to work well under pressure and maintain a professional and courteous work disposition; ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in handling sensitive information; ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.

The Green & Gold Congressional Aide Program (G&G CAP) was established to create employment opportunities for Veterans, Gold Star families, and Active-Duty spouses within the U.S. House of Representatives. These positions provide Aides with the experience and exposure necessary to broaden their career opportunities. G&G CAP Aides may be assigned to Members, Committees, or House Officers and will serve in Washington, DC or Member district offices across the country. These are two-year, paid, full-time employment opportunities.

Duties will vary depending upon the specific requirements for each office. Said duties may include but are not limited to:

Working as a constituent services representative;
Helping local constituents resolve issues with federal agencies;
Serving as liaison to local Veterans Service Organizations;
Attending local events and meetings on behalf of the Member of Congress; and
Performing legislative work.

SPECIFIC DUTIES FOR EACH OFFICE WILL BE DISCUSSED DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS. 

IN ORDER TO PARTICIPATE YOU MUST BE A VETERAN, GOLD STAR FAMILY MEMBER, OR ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSE AND MEET ALL THE FOLLOWING REQUIREMENTS. NO WAIVERS WILL BE GRANTED:


Veterans:


1.  Honorably discharged;

2.  Released from active duty within the last six years;

3.  Pay grades at or below E-5/O-3/W-2;

Veterans promoted to the pay grades of E-6/O-4/W-3 WITHIN 6-MONTHS OF SEPARATION from active duty may be eligible if they meet ALL other requirements.

4.  Veterans who are in receipt of a 20-year or Temporary Early Retirement Authority (TERA) 	    retirement are not eligible for the program.

Gold Star Families: 

A surviving spouse, child/step-child, parent/step-parent, or sibling/step-sibling of a veteran who died:
In the line of duty while serving in the Armed Forces; or
from a service-connected disability.

Active-Duty Spouses: 


Must be the spouse of an Active-Duty service member. Active-Duty service members work for the military full-time and are subject to permanent change of station or permanent change of assignment orders upon completion of each tour of duty.
Those subject to Title X mobilization are not eligible



Additional Requirements
Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. Ability to work well under pressure and maintain a professional and courteous work disposition; ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in handling sensitive information; ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. High School Diploma required.  Effective verbal and written communication skills are necessary.

Required Documents
VETERANS must submit a Resume and DD-214 (Member COPY 4)

GOLD STAR applicants require a Resume and one of the following:
**For members who died on active duty: DD-1300 or DD-2064, accompanied by a Line of Duty Determination; OR
**For members who died of service-connected disability: Last DD-214 issued to the veteran, and VA eligibility letter.
**Proof of dependency on the Veteran: DD-1172 (PAGE 2); OR
**Proof of relationship (obituary, birth certificate, etc…) to the Veteran.

ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSES must submit a Resume and a copy of the FRONT and BACK of Dependent ID card



Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.

  • A surviving spouse, child/step-child, parent/step-parent, or sibling/step-sibling of a veteran who died:
    • In the line of duty while serving in the Armed Forces; or

Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. These positions provide Aides with the experience and exposure necessary to broaden their career opportunities. G&G CAP Aides may be assigned to Members, Committees, or House Officers and will serve in Washington, DC or Member district offices across the country. These are two-year, paid, full-time employment opportunities.

Duties will vary depending upon the specific requirements for each office. Said duties may include but are not limited to:

  • Working as a constituent services representative;
  • Helping local constituents resolve issues with federal agencies;
  • Serving as liaison to local Veterans Service Organizations;
  • Attending local events and meetings on behalf of the Member of Congress; and
  • Performing legislative work.

SPECIFIC DUTIES FOR EACH OFFICE WILL BE DISCUSSED DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS. 

IN ORDER TO PARTICIPATE YOU MUST BE A VETERAN, GOLD STAR FAMILY MEMBER, OR ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSE AND MEET ALL THE FOLLOWING REQUIREMENTS. NO WAIVERS WILL BE GRANTED:

Veterans:

1.  Honorably discharged;

2.  Released from active duty within the last six years;

3.  Pay grades at or below E-5/O-3/W-2;

  • Veterans promoted to the pay grades of E-6/O-4/W-3 WITHIN 6-MONTHS OF SEPARATION from active duty may be eligible if they meet ALL other requirements.

4.  Veterans who are in receipt of a 20-year or Temporary Early Retirement Authority (TERA)     retirement are not eligible for the program.

Gold Star Families: 

  • A surviving spouse, child/step-child, parent/step-parent, or sibling/step-sibling of a veteran who died:
    • In the line of duty while serving in the Armed Forces; or
    • from a service-connected disability.

Active-Duty Spouses: 

  • Must be the spouse of an Active-Duty service member. Active-Duty service members work for the military full-time and are subject to permanent change of station or permanent change of assignment orders upon completion of each tour of duty.
    • Those subject to Title X mobilization are not eligible

Additional Requirements
Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. Ability to work well under pressure and maintain a professional and courteous work disposition; ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in handling sensitive information; ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. High School Diploma required.  Effective verbal and written communication skills are necessary.

Required Documents
VETERANS must submit a Resume and DD-214 (Member COPY 4)

GOLD STAR applicants require a Resume and one of the following:
**For members who died on active duty: DD-1300 or DD-2064, accompanied by a Line of Duty Determination; OR
**For members who died of service-connected disability: Last DD-214 issued to the veteran, and VA eligibility letter.
**Proof of dependency on the Veteran: DD-1172 (PAGE 2); OR
**Proof of relationship (obituary, birth certificate, etc…) to the Veteran.

ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSES must submit a Resume and a copy of the FRONT and BACK of Dependent ID card

Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.

Must be the spouse of an Active-Duty service member. Active-Duty service members work for the military full-time and are subject to permanent change of station or permanent change of assignment orders upon completion of each tour of duty.
Those subject to Title X mobilization are not eligible



Additional Requirements
Detail oriented; excellent organization skills; strong computer skills; ability to complete projects within timelines and under minimal supervision. Ability to work well under pressure and maintain a professional and courteous work disposition; ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment in handling sensitive information; ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. High School Diploma required.  Effective verbal and written communication skills are necessary.

Required Documents
VETERANS must submit a Resume and DD-214 (Member COPY 4)

GOLD STAR applicants require a Resume and one of the following:
**For members who died on active duty: DD-1300 or DD-2064, accompanied by a Line of Duty Determination; OR
**For members who died of service-connected disability: Last DD-214 issued to the veteran, and VA eligibility letter.
**Proof of dependency on the Veteran: DD-1172 (PAGE 2); OR
**Proof of relationship (obituary, birth certificate, etc…) to the Veteran.

ACTIVE-DUTY SPOUSES must submit a Resume and a copy of the FRONT and BACK of Dependent ID card



Please do not contact or submit applications to congressional offices.
